name: Iola Brubeck
abstract: David Warren Brubeck (; December 6, 1920 – December 5, 2012) was an American jazz pianist and composer. Often regarded as a foremost exponent of cool jazz, Brubeck's work is characterized by unusual time signatures and superimposing contrasting rhythms, meters, and tonalities, and combining different styles and genres, such as classical,…

interviews.title: Iola Brubeck interviewed by Monk Rowe, Wilton, CT, July 17, 2011

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Brubeck
about_quotes.evidence: My wife was driving... I said to Iola... Dave Brubeck and Iola Whitlock started their musical life together.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Brubeck interviewed by Monk Rowe, Wilton, Connecticut, November 21, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Ran Blake
about_quotes.evidence: my first time I ever got on the airplane Jeanne Lee was with me and in front of me was Iola Brubeck. And she said, “Kids, don’t worry, we’re not going to crash.” ... And Iola was wonderful.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ran Blake interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, July 16, 2021
